{{Quote|Our history is a saga of foresight, daring, and extravagant riches.|Lingsnot describing Bespin|<ref name="Illustrated" />}}
'''Po Ruddle Lingsnot''' was an [[Alien/Legends|alien]] used–[[Cloud car/Legends|cloud car]] salesman who was elected to the [[Council of Tourism and Extra-Planetary Investment]] of his home [[City/Legends|city]], [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. Also a member of Cloud City's ruling [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucratic]] class, the [[Exex]], Lingsnot extolled Bespin's virtues and hoped to eventually become Cloud City's [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|The overall concern of the Exex—as of politicians everywhere—is to maintain the status quo and to eliminate drastic changes for the citizenry.|Lingsnot, an Exex himself, describing his group|<ref name="Illustrated" />}}
Po Ruddle Lingsnot was a [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] individual<ref name="Illustrated" /> who lived in<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|257|Lingsnot, Po Ruddle}}</ref> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin's]] [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]]. Lingsnot was a used–[[Cloud car/Legends|cloud car]] salesman who got himself elected to the [[Council of Tourism and Extra-Planetary Investment]] and became an established member of Cloud City's ruling [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucratic]] class, the [[Exex]]. He wrote a report describing Bespin at some point after [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] became the [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of Cloud City<ref name="Illustrated" /> in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]. {{Tales|3|Lady Luck (comic story)}} shows [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] becoming [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] later, which corresponds to [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] per ''The New Essential Chronology''.</ref>

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Get carried away on the winds!|Lingsnot concluding his report|<ref name="Illustrated" />}}
[[File:Lando_Cloud_City_EotECR.png|thumb|right|300px|Lando Calrissian, Baron Administrator of Cloud City, whose title was sought by Lingsnot]]
Lingsnot worked hard to get elected to the Council of Tourism and Extra-Planetary Investment.<ref name="CSWE" /> A tireless promoter, the councilman would not be content just attending meetings or remaining in his plush offices but instead attended all vital civic events with a smiling welcome for all visiting interplanetary dignitaries. He openly aimed to be Cloud City's next Baron Administrator.<ref name="Illustrated" />

Lingsnot always extolled Bespin's virtues, and his report presented the same enthusiasm. Lingsnot expressed certain opinions in writing his detailed report, noting that it was sad that Cloud City's unlicensed [[Port Town (Cloud City)/Legends|Port Town]] [[Casino/Legends|casinos]] frequently preyed on financially desperate individuals, and writing that the [[City/Legends|city]] was at a loss for a solution. In discussing the planet's creatures, he considered [[Thranta/Legends|thrantas]] some of the most interesting, and he also wrote that it was wise for Cloud City to forbid hunting [[Beldon/Legends|beldons]] since that [[Species/Legends|species]] was speculated to exhale [[Tibanna/Legends|tibanna]], a [[Gas/Legends|gas]] essential for Cloud City's economy. He had [[Color/Legends|tan]] skin.<ref name="Illustrated" />

==Equipment==
Lingsnot wore a white [[Robe/Legends|robe]] over a blue shirt and donned a golden necklace.<ref name="Illustrated" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Monsters_and_Aliens_Adnerem.jpg|thumb|left|The rightmost character in this non-canon image of the Lingsnots was the basis for Po Ruddle Lingsnot.]]
"The [[Lingsnots]]" were created for [[1993]]'s ''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]'', written by [[Bob Carrau]]. Those images accompanied a holiday newsletter where the Lingsnots wrote about their children, [[Ussto Lingsnot|Ussto]], [[Miiliki-spoorr Lingsnot|Miiliki-spoorr]], and [[Ciption Lingsnot|Ciption]], over the past year.<ref name="MaAfGL">''[[Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas]]''</ref> [[Lucasfilm]] employee [[Leland Chee]], the person in charge of the [[Holocron continuity database]], indicated that information contained in ''Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas'' is [[Canon|non-canonical]]<ref name="Chee">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=And is information from Monsters and Aliens from George Lucas canon? Unless referred to by a source that is in-continuity, the information is non-continuity.}}</ref> with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="Legends">{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}}</ref> The book's illustration that would be later used to represent Po Ruddle Lingsnot [[in-universe]] is [[Nilo Rodis-Jamero]]'s concept art for the character [[Bib Fortuna/Legends|Bib Fortuna]] in the [[1983]] [[original trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="Article">{{SW|url=news/back-from-the-drawing-board-recycled-star-wars-aliens|text=Back from the Drawing Board: Recycled ''Star Wars'' Aliens|int=Back from the Drawing Board|nolive=1}}</ref>

In [[November]] [[1995]],<ref name="Year by Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> Po Ruddle Lingsnot was brought into the ''Star Wars'' Legends continuity by [[Kevin J. Anderson]]'s ''[[The Illustrated Star Wars Universe]]''.<ref name="Illustrated" /> Prior to that, in the summer of the same year,<ref name="Date">The "[[Star News]]" and "[[Lucasfilm's Latest]]" departments of [[Star Wars Insider 26|''Star Wars Insider'' 26]] collectively indicate that this issue was published between [[June 4]] and [[July 28]], [[1995]]. Given that ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' was published quarterly during this time, this indicates a summer 1995 release for issue 26.</ref> Lingsnot was mentioned, unnamed, in "[[The Write Stuff (Star Wars Insider 26)|The Write Stuff]]," an article that was published in the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine's [[Star Wars Insider 26|twenty-sixth issue]]. With that being Lingsnot's first mention in canonical—to the Legends continuity—''[[Star Wars]]'' media,<ref name="Insider">'{{InsiderCite|26|The Write Stuff (Star Wars Insider 26)}}</ref> [[October]] 1995's<ref name="GalaxyMagDate">[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 5|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 5]] indicates it was published in fall [[1995]], and as issues [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|three]] and [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|seven]] of ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]'' were published in [[April]] 1995 and [[1996]] respectively—according to {{GalaxyCite|2|Rebel Report}} and {{GalaxyCite|6|Around the Galaxy}} respectively—issue 5 must have been published in [[October]] 1995.</ref> "[[Another Spin Around the Galaxy]]," published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 5|issue five]] of the ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]'', was the first licensed source to use Po Ruddle Lingsnot's name prior to his in-universe introduction in ''The Illustrated Star Wars Universe''.<ref name="Galaxy">{{GalaxyCite|5|Another Spin Around the Galaxy}}</ref>
